name: Job Aggregator Visibility
description:
Hi team,

Please work on this request. Here are details for this task:

Scope: Careers Page Optimization for Job Aggregator Visibility

  1. Implement JobPosting Schema
    • Add JSON-LD JobPosting schema to job detail pages following Google guidelines
    • Ensure required fields are mapped:
      • title
      • description
      • datePosted
      • employmentType
      • hiringOrganization
      • jobLocation
      • validThrough (if applicable)
    • Validate using Rich Results Test / Schema Validator
  2. Automate Schema Injection
    • Integrate schema generation into CMS (StackShift) job post template
    • Auto-populate schema fields based on CMS inputs
    • Ensure schema is rendered server-side or on page load (crawlable)
  3. Create Dedicated Job Pages
    • Implement dynamic routing for individual job postings (e.g., /careers/[slug])
    • Each job must have:
      • Unique URL
      • Indexable page (no noindex)
      • SEO-friendly metadata (title, description)
  4. Restructure Careers Section
    • Convert existing Careers page into:
      • Main Careers Landing Page (list of open roles)
      • Individual Job Detail Pages
    • Landing page should:
      • List all active jobs
      • Link to each job detail page
      • Optionally include filters (location, role type)
  5. CMS Workflow for Job Posts
    • Create/confirm CMS model for job postings with required fields:
      • Job Title
      • Description (rich text)
      • Location
      • Employment Type
      • Posting Date
      • Expiry Date (optional)
    • Ensure easy creation/editing for new roles
    • Publishing a job = auto-generates page + schema
  6. QA & Validation
    • Test structured data on multiple job pages
    • Confirm pages are crawlable and indexed
    • Check internal linking from Careers page
    • Optional: Submit sitemap / request indexing

ETA
  • Estimated completion: 1–2 weeks

Let me know if there are any questions. Thank you!

name: StackShift UI Components Bugs
description:
Problem Statement

StackShift components fail to respect custom Tailwind CSS configurations defined in tailwind.config.js. When custom utility classes from extended theme values are applied via the className prop, the component's default variant styles take precedence, resulting in unintended style conflicts.

Example

Given a custom color extension in tailwind.config.js:

theme: {   extend: {     colors: {       cream: "#f5efe3",     }   } }

Applied to a Heading component:

<Heading   id="hero-headline"   type="h1"   weight="medium"   className="font-cream font-serif italic" >   {title} </Heading>

Current output:

<h1 class="text-primary false font-medium font-bold ... font-cream font-serif italic ...">

The text-primary default style is applied regardless of the custom className prop, causing style collisions.

Then can we remove any default styling if the <Button/> from stackshift/ui if the variant is "unstyled".

Hi Danessa,

I’m hoping you can help me. When we make changes to our website something keeps defaulting my title and my bio on the “Meet the Team” section.

 

Can you please change my title from Warehouse Manager to Vice President of Business and also change my Bio to the following

 

Justin was born and raised in St. Peters, Missouri, and grew up with a passion for sports, spending his youth playing football, hockey, and wrestling. After high school, he began his career in the collections industry, where he rose through the ranks from a collection agent to managing a facility of over 40 employees.

In 2019, Justin joined the Jamplast family as an Account Executive. Through his dedication and leadership, he advanced to oversee operations at the company’s production centers in Mount Vernon, IN, and Canton, OH. Today, Justin serves as Vice President of Business, helping to drive continued growth.

Justin and his wife Kathie reside in Evansville, Indiana. Outside of work, they enjoy traveling and spending time on the water boating.

 

Thank you!

 

 

Best Regards,

name: #19402 End of Engagement Effective 06/30/2025
description:
Hello Webriq Helpdesk and Alex Belding 😊

I’m writing to follow up on recent conversations, including my June 20th discussion with Alex Belding, to confirm that Jamplast INC will be concluding our engagement with WebriQ.

As of June 30, 2025, we will no longer require WebriQ’s services, as our transition to Odoo for website support is now complete. Please consider this message confirmation that services will end on that date, and we do not expect to receive invoices or charges beyond June.

We want to sincerely thank the WebriQ team for over 8 years of support. Your team has always been on the forefront of innovation and has helped us scale in numerous ways over the years. As our business grows and evolves — especially with new ventures in the B2C space — we’ll absolutely keep the door open for future collaboration. WebriQ will remain a top consideration as we explore opportunities in AI, enhanced front-end experiences, and advanced digital capabilities.

Alex, I started looking into the email you sent yesterday. That’s very interesting and we would like a trial work of that. Our IT guy will dig it.

Additionally, we’d be happy to extend referrals for WebriQ to others — your team’s reliability and technical leadership has always stood out.

  • Next week we are planning on migrating the website Jamplast and DiverterPlus. We may need some assistance to close out that last portion of the transition.

Please confirm receipt and acknowledge- also let us know if there are any final steps needed on our end.

Return and Restock Policy

name: (Urgent Review) Dynamic Logic Update for Member Signup Form
description:
We'd like to update the New Member Signup flow to support two different referral pathways:
1. Standard affiliate referrals (each affiliate has their own unique link with a ?ref=REFCODE)
2. Promotional/gifting referrals where many people will use the same promo code (e.g., holiday campaigns)

These two pathways have different requirements, so the best way I could think we could make this work is if the signup form adapts dynamically based on the referral code being used.

I'm open to ideas if ya have a better idea.  Jun really gets this so I really respect his input and buyin.

Here's what we're thinking:



1. Hide the Referral Code Field

Daniel helped me realize the current referral code field brings no value to the applicant and should be completely invisible to the user.
We only use the code for backend tracking and approval logic; it provides no value to the member experience and hiding it would ensure no unnecessary confusion.

This field should still:
• Auto-populate when a user lands on the page via a ?ref= parameter
• Validate internally just as it does now
• Remain fully functional, just not visible


2. New Conditional Field: “Who introduced you to Always?”

We are adding a new required field to the form:

Field label:
Who introduced you to Always?

Helper text:
Please share the name of the member who provided your product or link.

However, this field should not always appear. It is only needed for promotional scenarios.

So we're going to need some additional logic.

3. Dynamic Logic Based on Referral Code

We want the form to display this new field only when certain referral codes are used, specifically promo codes that will be distributed widely (e.g., holiday campaigns).

The logic should be:
• If the referral code in the URL matches a promo code (e.g., HOLIDAY25, GIFT2025, etc.):
→ Show the “Who introduced you to Always?” field (required field for submission)
• If the referral code in the URL is an affiliate’s unique code:
→ Hide the “Who introduced you to Always?” field completely (not a required field for submission)


LASTLY...
As to how we can implement this so we can easily mark which referral codes are considered “promo codes.”  So we don't have to have you update logic per promotion... well, that's the part I don't know.

Is there a way to generate referral codes (links) without having to create an "Affiliate Account"?  For example, SPEAKEASY25 is the generic referral code we use on a fake Affiliate we set up to get all our old members over to the new site.  This worked well as a band-aid solution, but could there be another way to generate these non-affiliate-related codes?

I asked AI to help with this and it talked about adding "a boolean flag in your admin interface like requiresReferrerName: true."  Which is said would "give us flexibility for future promotions without needing further code changes".  But I don't think that's it... at least it's not the whole picture, because we would want the promotional referral code to act like an Affiliate Code... this provides a way of tracking the effectiveness of the campaign (just like the effectiveness of an Affiliate).

Anyway, would love to know your thoughts on this ASAP.  We're already preparing to create some product bundles for Xmas and reaching out to our current customers to sell them on gifting our products this year. 

When gift recipiants of our product follow a Call To Action that leads them to creating a membership, we'd like that HOLIDAY25 code to trigger tha new field, so we can cross-reference the member that gifted it to them and approve their account right away.

As always, thanks for helping us scale the system for our unique use and needs.  You guys are the best!​

name: Waiver Content
description:
Hi Ulrick Sanchez ,

Here are updates and requests for the waiver page:

1. First, let's update the copy.
Here is what to swap it out with:

Always™ Event Participation & Personal Responsibility Agreement

Some experiences at this event may include optional, complimentary plant-based entheogenic supplements offered to gently support your mind, your body, and your sense of connection.

To ensure a safe experience and protect the well-being of our community, please carefully read and consent to the agreement below.

⸻

1. Personal Responsibility

I understand that I am responsible for my own physical, emotional, and spiritual well-being. My participation is my fully informed choice.

I accept full responsibility for the outcomes, sensations, and experiences that may arise during the event, as well as any immediate or long-term effects.

⸻

2. Voluntary Participation

I understand that all supplements provided by Always™ at this event are optional.
I may decline or discontinue at any point.

⸻

3. Non-Medical Nature

I acknowledge that Always™, its founders, facilitators, event hosts, and venue partners are not medical professionals, and that nothing in this experience constitutes medical advice, diagnosis, or treatment.

All offerings are for educational, personal-growth, and spiritual purposes only.
I agree to consult my own healthcare provider for any medical concerns or questions.

⸻

4. Health Considerations & Risk Factors

I understand that certain health conditions and medications may increase risk when combined with entheogenic or serotonergic substances. I agree to review the following information carefully and consult a qualified healthcare provider if I have any questions or uncertainties.

DROPDOWNS REMAIN (NO CHANGES)


I affirm that I have reviewed this information and agree that my participation is safe and appropriate for me.

⸻

5. Assumption of Risk & Release of Liability

I understand that engaging with plant-based entheogenic substances may result in potentially unexpected emotional, physical, or psychological effects. I knowingly and voluntarily assume all associated risks.

I release Always™, its founders, facilitators, event hosts, venue partners, and affiliates from all liability for claims, injuries, or outcomes arising from my participation — whether known or unforeseen.

⸻

6. Legal Terms

This agreement is governed by the laws of the State of Colorado. If any portion is deemed invalid, the remainder shall remain enforceable.

By signing below, I affirm that I have read, understood, and willingly accept the terms of this agreement.

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Next in importance is how the screen confirms the waiver is signed.  They are to turn the phone around and show this to our staff.  So I needs to be more minimal, so I've cut a lot out.  I just want to confirm that they used a real name, real email, and that they are over 21.

So if we could do something like this... with instructions at the top.
A message that they're all set.
The three main detials bigger and bolder for us to see.
This form submits slowly, confirms slowly, so maybe leaving the signature out of it will speed it up?  It's only necessary for us to see/store anyway.
And the "What's next" blue box...
It can just reitterate that they're all set, turn the phone around, and if you opted-in you'll be getting an email from us.
Remove Return to Home button at the bottom.  Don't want them to leave this page until they have their samples.



OTHER CHANGES:
1. Please have the Option already checked.  They can choose to uncheck or Opt-Out if they do not want the guide, and to receive communication from us.  But have it default to being checked please.

2. This will almost exclusively be used on people's phones.  So the mobile design and response is what I'm focused on most.  When the confirmation page appears on my phone it only shows the footer.  I have to scroll up.  Can you be sure it looks like the above confirmation image on the phone so they are not confused and can simply turn the phone around.  It will be anchored perfectly.

3. Do NOT send a confirmation email.  Unnecessary.  Let's hold the new contacts in our CRM, and once we have everything set up in Klaviyo or PublishForge.

description:

I don't understand why these kinds of problems are happening, and I can only do so much to troubleshoot.  So here's what I did (which resulted in the discovery of another critical problem):

I applied for a new member account via an affiliate link.

nathan@hildreth.co applied for a membership that nate.affiliate@always.love approved from their Account/Referral Program (backoffice).

The account shows approved to:
- The Affiliate in their backoffice.
- Admin in the Management Dashboard

However, nathan@hildreth.co did not receive an approval email.  Only an email acknowledging his submission (e.g. "While your submission is being reviewed")

Therefore, this new member could not log in, and could not change the password.
